channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Huawei succeeds where Microsoft failed miserably — HarmonyOS now on almost one billion devices, and China's largest mobile phone manufacturer has completely eliminated Android
item.description: Huawei’s mobile OS is on nearly a billion devices in China, and its new HarmonyOS Next removes all traces of Android.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams) , TechRadar

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Monday, July 1 (game #386)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren) , TechRadar

item.title: Abandoning doggy doo? DNA will sniff you out and expose you
item.description: Some apartment complexes and homeowners associations are requiring residents to submit DNA samples from their dogs to track who is responsible for piles of dog waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: TSA launches 3D scanners to detect explosives
item.description: The new technology will give agents a 3D image that can be turned 360 degrees for enhanced threat protection.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Video assistant referees to smart rings: Here’s the soccer tech at Euro 2024
item.description: From smart-enabled match balls and artificial intelligence to cryogenic recovery chambers, soccer is being transformed by the cutting-edge technology available to players, coaches and officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: FCC proposes rule requiring carriers to unlock all cellphones
item.description: The FCC wants carriers to unlock cellphones within 60 days of purchase so users can shop different networks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: What is a passkey? Why Apple is betting on password-free tech
item.description: When iOS 18 debuts in September, Apple will bring myriad new security features to users. But the company will also debut a new API for app and website developers that will protect users in more fundamental way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Opinion: Big tech companies are burning through reservoirs of trust with AI
item.description: Is generative AI a useful productivity tool or a series of overhyped, intrusive products that consume vital resources?
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to find and search YouTube video transcripts on your phone
item.description: YouTube videos are an amazing source of knowledge. But they’re often long, and trying to find a specific shred of info within an hour-long video isn’t exactly a great use of your time. Google will soon let some people use its Gemini AI tool to ask questions about a YouTube video on an Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: States Prepare Workers for AI-Driven Job Market Shift
item.description: As artificial intelligence gains traction in workplaces, states are proactively helping workers upskill to remain relevant, anticipating a future where many jobs will rely on generative AI and machines outsmart outdated skills.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon Investigates Claims of Improper Online Content Scraping by Perplexity AI
item.description: Amazon reviews allegations that Perplexity AI, an AI startup, is scraping online content including news site articles without permission.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury Pescadero Mansion Listed for $8 Million - A Solarium, Indoor Pool, and More
item.description: Discover this stunning 6,000-square-foot estate featuring a two-story solarium, indoor swimming pool with underwater channel to the outdoor lap pool, and more. This luxury property is now listed for sale at $8 million.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DoD’s Fulcrum to serve as north star for its IT objectives
item.description: “I want [Fulcrum] to get all the way down to the individual program managers and have them adjust their day-to-day decisions," said Leslie Beavers. The post DoD’s Fulcrum to serve as north star for its IT objectives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Massachusetts settles lawsuit with Uber and Lyft, guarantees minimum pay and benefits for drivers
item.description: Uber and Lyft agree to provide minimum wage, paid sick leave, and other benefits for Massachusetts drivers as part of a $175 million settlement resolving a multi-year legal battle
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: European Union's competition boss signals fresh AI scrutiny for Microsoft-OpenAI deal and Google
item.description: The European Union is escalating its scrutiny of the artificial intelligence industry, including taking a fresh look into Microsoft’s multibillion-dollar partnership with OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU commissioner slams Apple Intelligence delay
item.description: If you believe European Commission Vice President Margrethe Vestager, Apple’s decision to delay introduction of its Apple Intelligence AI is a “stunning declaration” of its own anticompetitive behavior.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: MicroStrategy boosts HyperIntelligence with artificial intelligence
item.description: MicroStrategy has added generative AI capabilities to HyperIntelligence, part of its One business intelligence platform, making it possible for workers to access data using natural language by asking questions from within any web application.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Hacienda se arma con IA para luchar contra el fraude
item.description: La Aministración pública acompasa cada vez más su transformación digital al ritmo que lleva el sector privado en su adopción de nuevas tecnologías. Es así como la inteligencia artificial (IA) se ha insertado en todo tipo de administración y servicio público en el último año.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Marine Corps enlists RPA, 5G, and AR/VR to retool fighting force
item.description: Ensuring the US Marine Corps is adequately outfitted with the right equipment, in top shape, even as its fighting needs evolve is no small feat. Today, that operation, undertaken by the US Marine Depot Maintenance Command (MDMC), is better oiled thanks to a transformation grounded in IT.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Box announces upgrade to Box AI, integration with GPT-4o
item.description: Box has unveiled a new set of features in Box AI that includes an integration with GPT-4o, support for image and spreadsheet files and the Box AI for Metadata API.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Broadcom bolsters VMware Edge Compute Stack
item.description: A slew of updates in VMware Edge Compute Stack (ECS) 3.5 are aimed at helping customers more easily manage edge devices, applications, and infrastructure across multiple locations, and industry watchers say the updated platform will support AI at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NASA Astronauts Delayed at Space Station Following Boeing Starliner Launch Issues
item.description: Two NASA astronauts are extending their stay at the International Space Station as NASA and Boeing work to resolve technical problems encountered during the spacecraft's journey to the orbiting laboratory.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pure Storage Leverages Artificial Intelligence for Enhanced Security and Performance in Storage Solutions
item.description: Pure Storage, a leading provider of storage solutions, has unveiled new AI-driven features designed to boost security and performance for AI workloads and detect ransomware attacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Pope Francis Decrees Vatican Radio Territory in Northern Rome to be Repurposed into Solar Farm
item.description: Pope Francis has decreed that a territory in northern Rome, previously criticized for emitting electromagnetic waves from Vatican Radio towers, will now house a field of solar panels to power Vatican City, aligning with the Vatican's pledge at the UN
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised the national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UN Launches Global Principles to Combat Online Hate, Demands Big Tech Action
item.description: The United Nations launches global principles to combat online hate and lies, demanding big tech companies take immediate action to reduce harm caused to individuals and societies worldwide.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Music record labels sue AI song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Big record companies are suing artificial intelligence song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement, alleging that the AI music startups are exploiting the recorded works of artists from Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple becomes first target of EU's new digital competition rules aimed at big tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators on Monday leveled their first charges under the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook, accusing Apple of preventing app makers from pointing users to cheaper options outside its App Store.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
